'''天曽利命'''は[[隼人]]族の[[祖神]]であったとされる人物<ref name=":0">{{Cite web |title=A109 天曽利命 |url=https://www.his-trip.info/keizu/entry46.html |website=www.his-trip.info |access-date=2023-12-14 |language=ja}}</ref><ref name=":1">{{Cite web |title=�v�Ď��l |url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160319134404/http://ek1010.sakura.ne.jp/1234-7-41.html |website=web.archive.org |date=2016-03-19 |access-date=2024-01-06}}</ref><ref name=":2">{{Cite web |title=【ﾑｰﾀﾞﾝ】李氏朝鮮VS琉球王国7【ﾕﾀ】 |url=https://webcache.googleusercontent.com/search?q=cache:27J1abZ5nb0J:mimizun.com/log/2ch/korea/1347841276/ |website=webcache.googleusercontent.com |access-date=2024-01-06}}</ref><ref>{{Cite book |title=古代氏族系譜集成 |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=EL0nAAAAYAAJ&newbks=0&printsec=frontcover&dq=%22%E5%A4%A9%E6%9B%BD%E5%88%A9%E5%91%BD%22&q=%22%E5%A4%A9%E6%9B%BD%E5%88%A9%E5%91%BD%22&hl=en |publisher=古代氏族研究会 |date=1986 |language=zh-TW |first=Toshio |last=Hōga |last2=宝賀寿男}}</ref><ref>[https://ndlsearch.ndl.go.jp/books/R100000002-I000002274014 古代豪族系図集覧]</ref>。
彼は[[神武天皇]]の最初の妻である[[吾平津媛]]の兄だと言われています<ref name=":0" />。
天曽利命は時々、吾田君小橋と同一視されることがあります<ref name=":1" />。
天曽利命は[[火酢芹命]]の息子である可能性があります<ref name=":2" />。
== 系譜 == {{日向三代}}

== 解説 == 彼は[[神武天皇]]の最初の妻である[[吾平津媛]]の兄だと言われています<ref name=":0" />。
天曽利命は時々、吾田君小橋と同一視されることがあります<ref name=":1" />。
天曽利命は[[火酢芹命]]の息子である可能性があります<ref name=":2" />。
== 系譜 == {{日向三代}}

revision.text:
[[ja:天曽利命]] Tensori no Mikoto (天曽利命) est considéré comme le {{ill|Oyagami|texte=dieu ancestral}} de la [[Hayato|tribu Hayato]]. <ref name="A109 天曽利命">{{Lien web |langue=ja |titre=A109 天曽利命 |url=https://www.his-trip.info/keizu/entry46.html |site=www.his-trip.info |consulté le=2023-12-14}}</ref> <ref name="sakura.ne.jp">{{Lien web |titre=�v�Ď��l |url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160319134404/http://ek1010.sakura.ne.jp/1234-7-41.html |site=web.archive.org |date=2016-03-19 |consulté le=2024-01-06}}</ref> <ref name="ﾑｰﾀﾞﾝ">{{Lien web |titre=【ﾑｰﾀﾞﾝ】李氏朝鮮VS琉球王国7【ﾕﾀ】 |url=https://webcache.googleusercontent.com/search?q=cache:27J1abZ5nb0J:mimizun.com/log/2ch/korea/1347841276/ |site=webcache.googleusercontent.com |consulté le=2024-01-06}}</ref> <ref>{{Ouvrage|langue=zh-TW|prénom1=Toshio|nom1=Hōga|nom2=宝賀寿男|titre=古代氏族系譜集成|éditeur=古代氏族研究会|date=1986|lire en ligne=https://books.google.com/books?id=EL0nAAAAYAAJ&newbks=0&printsec=frontcover&dq=%22%E5%A4%A9%E6%9B%BD%E5%88%A9%E5%91%BD%22&q=%22%E5%A4%A9%E6%9B%BD%E5%88%A9%E5%91%BD%22&hl=en}}</ref> <ref>[https://ndlsearch.ndl.go.jp/books/R100000002-I000002274014 古代豪族系図集覧]</ref>
=== Explication === Il est dit qu'il est le frère aîné d'Ahiratsu-hime (吾平津媛), la première épouse de l'empereur Jimmu<ref name="A109 天曽利命" />.
Tensori no Mikoto est parfois identifié à Agatanushi Obashi (吾田君小橋)<ref name="sakura.ne.jp" />.
Il est possible que Tensori no Mikoto soit le fils de {{ill|Hosuseri}}<ref name="ﾑｰﾀﾞﾝ" />. [[en:draft:Tensori no Mikoto]]
== Article connexe ==
* {{ill|Ahiratsu-hime}} * {{ill|Oyagami|texte=dieu ancestral}} * {{ill|Oyagami|texte=Divinités ancestrales}} du [[Peuple Azumi|clan Azumi]] ** {{ill|Utsushihikanasaku}} ** {{ill|Azumi-no-isora}}
== Les references ==

[[ja:天曽利命]] Tensori no Mikoto (天曽利命) est considéré comme le {{ill|Oyagami|texte=dieu ancestral}} de la [[Hayato|tribu Hayato]]. <ref name="A109 天曽利命">{{Lien web |langue=ja |titre=A109 天曽利命 |url=https://www.his-trip.info/keizu/entry46.html |site=www.his-trip.info |consulté le=2023-12-14}}</ref>{{,}}<ref name="sakura.ne.jp">{{Lien web |titre=�v�Ď��l |url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160319134404/http://ek1010.sakura.ne.jp/1234-7-41.html |site=web.archive.org |date=2016-03-19 |consulté le=2024-01-06}}</ref>{{,}}<ref name="ﾑｰﾀﾞﾝ">{{Lien web |titre=【ﾑｰﾀﾞﾝ】李氏朝鮮VS琉球王国7【ﾕﾀ】 |url=https://webcache.googleusercontent.com/search?q=cache:27J1abZ5nb0J:mimizun.com/log/2ch/korea/1347841276/ |site=webcache.googleusercontent.com |consulté le=2024-01-06}}</ref>{{,}}<ref>{{Ouvrage|langue=zh-TW|prénom1=Toshio|nom1=Hōga|nom2=宝賀寿男|titre=古代氏族系譜集成|éditeur=古代氏族研究会|date=1986|lire en ligne=https://books.google.com/books?id=EL0nAAAAYAAJ&newbks=0&printsec=frontcover&dq=%22%E5%A4%A9%E6%9B%BD%E5%88%A9%E5%91%BD%22&q=%22%E5%A4%A9%E6%9B%BD%E5%88%A9%E5%91%BD%22&hl=en}}</ref>{{,}}<ref>[https://ndlsearch.ndl.go.jp/books/R100000002-I000002274014 古代豪族系図集覧]</ref>
== Explication == Il est dit qu'il est le frère aîné d'Ahiratsu-hime (吾平津媛), la première épouse de l'empereur Jimmu<ref name="A109 天曽利命" />.
Tensori no Mikoto est parfois identifié à Agatanushi Obashi (吾田君小橋)<ref name="sakura.ne.jp" />.
Il est possible que Tensori no Mikoto soit le fils de {{ill|Hosuseri}}<ref name="ﾑｰﾀﾞﾝ" />. [[en:draft:Tensori no Mikoto]]
== Article connexe ==

revision.text:
{{複数の問題 | 精度 = 2024年2月2日 (金) 10:56 (UTC) | 正確性 = 2024年2月2日 (金) 10:56 (UTC) | Wikify = 2024年2月2日 (金) 10:56 (UTC) }}{{更新|date=2024年5月}}{{未検証|date=2024年2月}} {{Uncategorized|date=2024年2月}} '''天曽利命'''（てんそりのみこと）は[[隼人]]族の[[祖神]]であったとされる人物<ref name="A109 天曽利命">{{Cite web |title=A109 天曽利命 |url=https://www.his-trip.info/keizu/entry46.html |website=www.his-trip.info |access-date=2023-12-14 |language=ja}}</ref><ref name="sakura.ne.jp">{{Cite web |title=�v�Ď��l |url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160319134404/http://ek1010.sakura.ne.jp/1234-7-41.html |website=web.archive.org |date=2016-03-19 |access-date=2024-01-06}}</ref><ref name="ﾑｰﾀﾞﾝ">{{Cite web |title=【ﾑｰﾀﾞﾝ】李氏朝鮮VS琉球王国7【ﾕﾀ】 |url=https://webcache.googleusercontent.com/search?q=cache:27J1abZ5nb0J:mimizun.com/log/2ch/korea/1347841276/ |website=webcache.googleusercontent.com |access-date=2024-01-06}}</ref><ref>{{Cite book |title=古代氏族系譜集成 |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=EL0nAAAAYAAJ&newbks=0&printsec=frontcover&dq=%22%E5%A4%A9%E6%9B%BD%E5%88%A9%E5%91%BD%22&q=%22%E5%A4%A9%E6%9B%BD%E5%88%A9%E5%91%BD%22&hl=en |publisher=古代氏族研究会 |date=1986 |language=zh-TW |first=Toshio |last=Hōga |last2=宝賀寿男}}</ref><ref>[https://ndlsearch.ndl.go.jp/books/R100000002-I000002274014 古代豪族系図集覧]</ref>。
== 解説 == 彼は[[神武天皇]]の最初の妻である[[吾平津媛]]の兄だと言われています<ref name="A109 天曽利命" />。
天曽利命は時々、吾田君小橋と同一視されることがあります<ref name="sakura.ne.jp" />。
天曽利命は[[火酢芹命]]の息子である可能性があります<ref name="ﾑｰﾀﾞﾝ" />。
== 系譜 == {{日向三代}}
== 関連項目 ==
* [[吾平津媛]] * [[阿曇氏]]の[[祖神]] ** [[宇都志日金拆命]] ** [[阿曇磯良]]
== 脚注 == <references /> [[Category:日本の神 (人物神 記紀)]] [[Category:隼人]]

revision.text:
{{Infobox person | name = Tensori no Mikoto | native_name = 天曽利命 | children = {{ill|Hayato people|en}} | father = {{ill|Hoderi|en}}<ref>''Nihongi: Chronicles of Japan from the Earliest Times to A.D. 697'', translated from the original Chinese and Japanese by [[William George Aston]]. Book II, page 73. Tuttle Publishing. Tra edition (July 2005). First edition published 1972. {{ISBN|978-0-8048-3674-6}}</ref><ref>"According to the 'Kojiki', the great 8th century A.D. compilation of Japanese mythology, Konohana Sakuya-hime married a god who grew suspicious of her when she became pregnant shortly after their wedding. To prove her fidelity to her husband, she entered a benign [[Bedroom|bower]] and miraculously gave birth to a son, unscathed by the surrounding flames. The fire ceremony at Fuji-Yyoshida recalls this story as a means of protecting the town from fire and promoting easy childbirth among women."</ref> }}
'''Tensori no Mikoto''' (天曽利命) a Shinto god. He is considered the {{Interlanguage link|Oyagami}} or ancestral god of the {{Ill|Hayato people|en|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Hayato people}}<ref name="A109 天曽利命">{{Cite web|title=A109 天曽利命|url=https://www.his-trip.info/keizu/entry46.html|access-date=2023-12-14|website=www.his-trip.info|language=ja}}</ref><ref name="sakura.ne.jp">{{Cite web|date=2016-03-19|title=�v�Ď��l|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160319134404/http://ek1010.sakura.ne.jp/1234-7-41.html|access-date=2024-01-06|website=web.archive.org}}</ref><ref name="ﾑｰﾀﾞﾝ">{{Cite web|title=【ﾑｰﾀﾞﾝ】李氏朝鮮VS琉球王国7【ﾕﾀ】|url=https://webcache.googleusercontent.com/search?q=cache:27J1abZ5nb0J:mimizun.com/log/2ch/korea/1347841276/|access-date=2024-01-06|website=webcache.googleusercontent.com}}</ref><ref>{{Cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=EL0nAAAAYAAJ&newbks=0&printsec=frontcover&dq=%22%E5%A4%A9%E6%9B%BD%E5%88%A9%E5%91%BD%22&q=%22%E5%A4%A9%E6%9B%BD%E5%88%A9%E5%91%BD%22&hl=en|title=古代氏族系譜集成|last=Hōga|first=Toshio|last2=宝賀寿男|date=1986|publisher=古代氏族研究会|language=zh-TW}}</ref><ref>[https://ndlsearch.ndl.go.jp/books/R100000002-I000002274014 古代豪族系図集覧]</ref>.
This means the {{Ill|Hayato people|en|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Hayato people}} used to believe they were descended from him.
== Explanation == He is said to be the older brother of {{Interlanguage link|Ahiratsu-hime|4=en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Ahiratsu-hime}} (吾平津媛), the first wife of [[Emperor Jimmu]]. <ref name="A109 天曽利命">{{Cite web|title=A109 天曽利命|url=https://www.his-trip.info/keizu/entry46.html|access-date=2023-12-14|website=www.his-trip.info|language=ja}}</ref>
Some people think he had an another name: Agatanushi Obashi (吾田君小橋) <ref name="sakura.ne.jp">{{Cite web|date=2016-03-19|title=�v�Ď��l|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160319134404/http://ek1010.sakura.ne.jp/1234-7-41.html|access-date=2024-01-06|website=web.archive.org}}</ref> .
It is possible that Tensori no Mikoto is {{Interlanguage link|Hosuseri|4=en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Hosuseri}}'s son <ref name="ﾑｰﾀﾞﾝ">{{Cite web|title=【ﾑｰﾀﾞﾝ】李氏朝鮮VS琉球王国7【ﾕﾀ】|url=https://webcache.googleusercontent.com/search?q=cache:27J1abZ5nb0J:mimizun.com/log/2ch/korea/1347841276/|access-date=2024-01-06|website=webcache.googleusercontent.com}}</ref> .
== Family tree == {{Three generations of Hyuga}}
== Related articles ==

{{Infobox person | name = Tensori no Mikoto | native_name = 天曽利命 | children = {{ill|Hayato people|en}} | father = {{ill|Hoderi|en}}<ref>''Nihongi: Chronicles of Japan from the Earliest Times to A.D. 697'', translated from the original Chinese and Japanese by [[William George Aston]]. Book II, page 73. Tuttle Publishing. Tra edition (July 2005). First edition published 1972. {{ISBN|978-0-8048-3674-6}}</ref><ref>"According to the 'Kojiki', the great 8th century A.D. compilation of Japanese mythology, Konohana Sakuya-hime married a god who grew suspicious of her when she became pregnant shortly after their wedding. To prove her fidelity to her husband, she entered a benign [[Bedroom|bower]] and miraculously gave birth to a son, unscathed by the surrounding flames. The fire ceremony at Fuji-Yyoshida recalls this story as a means of protecting the town from fire and promoting easy childbirth among women."</ref> }}
'''Tensori no Mikoto''' (天曽利命) a Shinto god. He is considered the {{Interlanguage link|Oyagami}} or ancestral god of the {{Ill|Hayato people|en|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Hayato people}}<ref name="A109 天曽利命">{{Cite web|title=A109 天曽利命|url=https://www.his-trip.info/keizu/entry46.html|access-date=2023-12-14|website=www.his-trip.info|language=ja}}</ref><ref name="sakura.ne.jp">{{Cite web|date=2016-03-19|title=�v�Ď��l|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160319134404/http://ek1010.sakura.ne.jp/1234-7-41.html|access-date=2024-01-06|website=web.archive.org}}</ref><ref name="ﾑｰﾀﾞﾝ">{{Cite web|title=【ﾑｰﾀﾞﾝ】李氏朝鮮VS琉球王国7【ﾕﾀ】|url=https://webcache.googleusercontent.com/search?q=cache:27J1abZ5nb0J:mimizun.com/log/2ch/korea/1347841276/|access-date=2024-01-06|website=webcache.googleusercontent.com}}</ref><ref>{{Cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=EL0nAAAAYAAJ&newbks=0&printsec=frontcover&dq=%22%E5%A4%A9%E6%9B%BD%E5%88%A9%E5%91%BD%22&q=%22%E5%A4%A9%E6%9B%BD%E5%88%A9%E5%91%BD%22&hl=en|title=古代氏族系譜集成|last=Hōga|first=Toshio|last2=宝賀寿男|date=1986|publisher=古代氏族研究会|language=zh-TW}}</ref><ref>[https://ndlsearch.ndl.go.jp/books/R100000002-I000002274014 古代豪族系図集覧]</ref>.
This means the {{Ill|Hayato people|en|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Hayato people}} used to believe they were descended from him.
== Explanation == He is said to be the older brother of {{Interlanguage link|Ahiratsu-hime|4=en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Ahiratsu-hime}} (吾平津媛), the first wife of [[Emperor Jimmu]]. <ref name="A109 天曽利命">{{Cite web|title=A109 天曽利命|url=https://www.his-trip.info/keizu/entry46.html|access-date=2023-12-14|website=www.his-trip.info|language=ja}}</ref>
Some people think he had an another name: Agatanushi Obashi (吾田君小橋) <ref name="sakura.ne.jp">{{Cite web|date=2016-03-19|title=�v�Ď��l|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160319134404/http://ek1010.sakura.ne.jp/1234-7-41.html|access-date=2024-01-06|website=web.archive.org}}</ref> .
It is possible that Tensori no Mikoto is {{Interlanguage link|Hosuseri|4=en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Hosuseri}}'s son <ref name="ﾑｰﾀﾞﾝ">{{Cite web|title=【ﾑｰﾀﾞﾝ】李氏朝鮮VS琉球王国7【ﾕﾀ】|url=https://webcache.googleusercontent.com/search?q=cache:27J1abZ5nb0J:mimizun.com/log/2ch/korea/1347841276/|access-date=2024-01-06|website=webcache.googleusercontent.com}}</ref> .

{{Infobox person | name = Tensori no Mikoto | native_name = 天曽利命 | children = {{ill|Hayato people|en}} | father = {{ill|Hoderi|en}}<ref>''Nihongi: Chronicles of Japan from the Earliest Times to A.D. 697'', translated from the original Chinese and Japanese by [[William George Aston]]. Book II, page 73. Tuttle Publishing. Tra edition (July 2005). First edition published 1972. {{ISBN|978-0-8048-3674-6}}</ref><ref>"According to the 'Kojiki', the great 8th century A.D. compilation of Japanese mythology, Konohana Sakuya-hime married a god who grew suspicious of her when she became pregnant shortly after their wedding. To prove her fidelity to her husband, she entered a benign [[Bedroom|bower]] and miraculously gave birth to a son, unscathed by the surrounding flames. The fire ceremony at Fuji-Yyoshida recalls this story as a means of protecting the town from fire and promoting easy childbirth among women."</ref> }}
'''Tensori no Mikoto''' (天曽利命) a Shinto god. He is considered the {{Interlanguage link|Oyagami}} or ancestral god of the {{Ill|Hayato people|en|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Hayato people}}<ref name="A109 天曽利命">{{Cite web|title=A109 天曽利命|url=https://www.his-trip.info/keizu/entry46.html|access-date=2023-12-14|website=www.his-trip.info|language=ja}}</ref><ref name="sakura.ne.jp">{{Cite web|date=2016-03-19|title=�v�Ď��l|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160319134404/http://ek1010.sakura.ne.jp/1234-7-41.html|access-date=2024-01-06|website=web.archive.org}}</ref><ref name="ﾑｰﾀﾞﾝ">{{Cite web|title=【ﾑｰﾀﾞﾝ】李氏朝鮮VS琉球王国7【ﾕﾀ】|url=https://webcache.googleusercontent.com/search?q=cache:27J1abZ5nb0J:mimizun.com/log/2ch/korea/1347841276/|access-date=2024-01-06|website=webcache.googleusercontent.com}}</ref><ref>{{Cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=EL0nAAAAYAAJ&newbks=0&printsec=frontcover&dq=%22%E5%A4%A9%E6%9B%BD%E5%88%A9%E5%91%BD%22&q=%22%E5%A4%A9%E6%9B%BD%E5%88%A9%E5%91%BD%22&hl=en|title=古代氏族系譜集成|last=Hōga|first=Toshio|last2=宝賀寿男|date=1986|publisher=古代氏族研究会|language=zh-TW}}</ref><ref>[https://ndlsearch.ndl.go.jp/books/R100000002-I000002274014 古代豪族系図集覧]</ref>.
This means the {{Ill|Hayato people|en|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Hayato people}} used to believe they were descended from him.
== Explanation == He is said to be the older brother of {{Interlanguage link|Ahiratsu-hime|4=en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Ahiratsu-hime}} (吾平津媛), the first wife of [[Emperor Jimmu]]. <ref name="A109 天曽利命">{{Cite web|title=A109 天曽利命|url=https://www.his-trip.info/keizu/entry46.html|access-date=2023-12-14|website=www.his-trip.info|language=ja}}</ref>
Some people think he had an another name: Agatanushi Obashi (吾田君小橋) <ref name="sakura.ne.jp">{{Cite web|date=2016-03-19|title=�v�Ď��l|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160319134404/http://ek1010.sakura.ne.jp/1234-7-41.html|access-date=2024-01-06|website=web.archive.org}}</ref> .
It is possible that Tensori no Mikoto is {{Interlanguage link|Hosuseri|4=en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Hosuseri}}'s son <ref name="ﾑｰﾀﾞﾝ">{{Cite web|title=【ﾑｰﾀﾞﾝ】李氏朝鮮VS琉球王国7【ﾕﾀ】|url=https://webcache.googleusercontent.com/search?q=cache:27J1abZ5nb0J:mimizun.com/log/2ch/korea/1347841276/|access-date=2024-01-06|website=webcache.googleusercontent.com}}</ref> .

== Explanation == He is said to be the older brother of {{Interlanguage link|Ahiratsu-hime|4=en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Ahiratsu-hime}} (吾平津媛), the first wife of [[Emperor Jimmu]]. <ref name="A109 天曽利命">{{Cite web|title=A109 天曽利命|url=https://www.his-trip.info/keizu/entry46.html|access-date=2023-12-14|website=www.his-trip.info|language=ja}}</ref>
Some people think he had an another name: Agatanushi Obashi (吾田君小橋) <ref name="sakura.ne.jp">{{Cite web|date=2016-03-19|title=�v�Ď��l|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160319134404/http://ek1010.sakura.ne.jp/1234-7-41.html|access-date=2024-01-06|website=web.archive.org}}</ref> .
It is possible that Tensori no Mikoto is {{Interlanguage link|Hosuseri|4=en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Hosuseri}}'s son <ref name="ﾑｰﾀﾞﾝ">{{Cite web|title=【ﾑｰﾀﾞﾝ】李氏朝鮮VS琉球王国7【ﾕﾀ】|url=https://webcache.googleusercontent.com/search?q=cache:27J1abZ5nb0J:mimizun.com/log/2ch/korea/1347841276/|access-date=2024-01-06|website=webcache.googleusercontent.com}}</ref> .

Book II, page 73. Tuttle Publishing. Tra edition (July 2005). First edition published 1972. {{ISBN|978-0-8048-3674-6}}</ref><ref>"According to the 'Kojiki', the great 8th century A.D. compilation of Japanese mythology, Konohana Sakuya-hime married a god who grew suspicious of her when she became pregnant shortly after their wedding. To prove her fidelity to her husband, she entered a benign [[Bedroom|bower]] and miraculously gave birth to a son, unscathed by the surrounding flames. The fire ceremony at Fuji-Yyoshida recalls this story as a means of protecting the town from fire and promoting easy childbirth among women."</ref> }}

{{Infobox person | name = Tensori no Mikoto | native_name = 天曽利命 | children = {{ill|Hayato people|en}} | father = {{ill|Hoderi|en}}<ref>''Nihongi: Chronicles of Japan from the Earliest Times to A.D. 697'', translated from the original Chinese and Japanese by [[William George Aston]]. Book II, page 73. Tuttle Publishing. Tra edition (July 2005). First edition published 1972. {{ISBN|978-0-8048-3674-6}}</ref><ref>"According to the 'Kojiki', the great 8th century A.D. compilation of Japanese mythology, Konohana Sakuya-hime married a god who grew suspicious of her when she became pregnant shortly after their wedding. To prove her fidelity to her husband, she entered a benign [[Bedroom|bower]] and miraculously gave birth to a son, unscathed by the surrounding flames. The fire ceremony at Fuji-Yyoshida recalls this story as a means of protecting the town from fire and promoting easy childbirth among women."</ref> }}
'''Tensori no Mikoto''' (天曽利命) a Shinto god. He is considered the {{Interlanguage link|Oyagami}} or ancestral god of the {{Ill|Hayato people|en|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Hayato people}}<ref name="A109 天曽利命">{{Cite web|title=A109 天曽利命|url=https://www.his-trip.info/keizu/entry46.html|access-date=2023-12-14|website=www.his-trip.info|language=ja}}</ref><ref name="sakura.ne.jp">{{Cite web|date=2016-03-19|title=�v�Ď��l|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160319134404/http://ek1010.sakura.ne.jp/1234-7-41.html|access-date=2024-01-06|website=web.archive.org}}</ref><ref name="ﾑｰﾀﾞﾝ">{{Cite web|title=【ﾑｰﾀﾞﾝ】李氏朝鮮VS琉球王国7【ﾕﾀ】|url=https://webcache.googleusercontent.com/search?q=cache:27J1abZ5nb0J:mimizun.com/log/2ch/korea/1347841276/|access-date=2024-01-06|website=webcache.googleusercontent.com}}</ref><ref>{{Cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=EL0nAAAAYAAJ&newbks=0&printsec=frontcover&dq=%22%E5%A4%A9%E6%9B%BD%E5%88%A9%E5%91%BD%22&q=%22%E5%A4%A9%E6%9B%BD%E5%88%A9%E5%91%BD%22&hl=en|title=古代氏族系譜集成|last=Hōga|first=Toshio|last2=宝賀寿男|date=1986|publisher=古代氏族研究会|language=zh-TW}}</ref><ref>[https://ndlsearch.ndl.go.jp/books/R100000002-I000002274014 古代豪族系図集覧]</ref>.
This means the {{Ill|Hayato people|en|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Hayato people}} used to believe they were descended from him.
== Explanation == He is said to be the older brother of {{Interlanguage link|Ahiratsu-hime|4=en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Ahiratsu-hime}} (吾平津媛), the first wife of [[Emperor Jimmu]]. <ref name="A109 天曽利命">{{Cite web|title=A109 天曽利命|url=https://www.his-trip.info/keizu/entry46.html|access-date=2023-12-14|website=www.his-trip.info|language=ja}}</ref>
Some people think he had an another name: Agatanushi Obashi (吾田君小橋) <ref name="sakura.ne.jp">{{Cite web|date=2016-03-19|title=�v�Ď��l|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160319134404/http://ek1010.sakura.ne.jp/1234-7-41.html|access-date=2024-01-06|website=web.archive.org}}</ref> .
It is possible that Tensori no Mikoto is {{Interlanguage link|Hosuseri|4=en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Hosuseri}}'s son <ref name="ﾑｰﾀﾞﾝ">{{Cite web|title=【ﾑｰﾀﾞﾝ】李氏朝鮮VS琉球王国7【ﾕﾀ】|url=https://webcache.googleusercontent.com/search?q=cache:27J1abZ5nb0J:mimizun.com/log/2ch/korea/1347841276/|access-date=2024-01-06|website=webcache.googleusercontent.com}}</ref> .

This means the {{Ill|Hayato people|en|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Hayato people}} used to believe they were descended from him.
== Explanation == He is said to be the older brother of {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Ahiratsu-hime|4=en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Ahiratsu-hime}} (吾平津媛), the first wife of [[Emperor Jimmu]]. <ref name="A109 天曽利命">{{Cite web|title=A109 天曽利命|url=https://www.his-trip.info/keizu/entry46.html|access-date=2023-12-14|website=www.his-trip.info|language=ja}}</ref>
Some people think he had an another name: Agatanushi Obashi (吾田君小橋) <ref name="sakura.ne.jp">{{Cite web|date=2016-03-19|title=�v�Ď��l|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160319134404/http://ek1010.sakura.ne.jp/1234-7-41.html|access-date=2024-01-06|website=web.archive.org}}</ref> .
It is possible that Tensori no Mikoto is {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Hosuseri|4=en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Hosuseri}}'s son <ref name="ﾑｰﾀﾞﾝ">{{Cite web|title=【ﾑｰﾀﾞﾝ】李氏朝鮮VS琉球王国7【ﾕﾀ】|url=https://webcache.googleusercontent.com/search?q=cache:27J1abZ5nb0J:mimizun.com/log/2ch/korea/1347841276/|access-date=2024-01-06|website=webcache.googleusercontent.com}}</ref> .

{{Infobox person | name = Tensori no Mikoto | native_name = 天曽利命 | children = {{ill|Hayato people|en}} | father = {{ill|Hoderi|en}}<ref>''Nihongi: Chronicles of Japan from the Earliest Times to A.D. 697'', translated from the original Chinese and Japanese by [[William George Aston]]. Book II, page 73. Tuttle Publishing. Tra edition (July 2005). First edition published 1972. {{ISBN|978-0-8048-3674-6}}</ref><ref>"According to the 'Kojiki', the great 8th century A.D. compilation of Japanese mythology, Konohana Sakuya-hime married a god who grew suspicious of her when she became pregnant shortly after their wedding. To prove her fidelity to her husband, she entered a benign [[Bedroom|bower]] and miraculously gave birth to a son, unscathed by the surrounding flames. The fire ceremony at Fuji-Yyoshida recalls this story as a means of protecting the town from fire and promoting easy childbirth among women."</ref> }}

{{Infobox person | name = Tensori no Mikoto | native_name = 天曽利命 | children = {{ill|Hayato people|en}} | father = {{ill|Hoderi|en}}<ref>''Nihongi: Chronicles of Japan from the Earliest Times to A.D. 697'', translated from the original Chinese and Japanese by [[William George Aston]]. Book II, page 73. Tuttle Publishing. Tra edition (July 2005). First edition published 1972. {{ISBN|978-0-8048-3674-6}}</ref><ref>"According to the 'Kojiki', the great 8th century A.D. compilation of Japanese mythology, Konohana Sakuya-hime married a god who grew suspicious of her when she became pregnant shortly after their wedding. To prove her fidelity to her husband, she entered a benign [[Bedroom|bower]] and miraculously gave birth to a son, unscathed by the surrounding flames. The fire ceremony at Fuji-Yyoshida recalls this story as a means of protecting the town from fire and promoting easy childbirth among women."</ref> }}
'''Tensori no Mikoto''' (天曽利命) a Shinto god. He is considered the {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Oyagami}} or ancestral god of the {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Hayato people|en|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Hayato people}}<ref name="A109 天曽利命">{{Cite web|title=A109 天曽利命|url=https://www.his-trip.info/keizu/entry46.html|access-date=2023-12-14|website=www.his-trip.info|language=ja}}</ref><ref name="sakura.ne.jp">{{Cite web|date=2016-03-19|title=�v�Ď��l|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160319134404/http://ek1010.sakura.ne.jp/1234-7-41.html|access-date=2024-01-06|website=web.archive.org}}</ref><ref name="ﾑｰﾀﾞﾝ">{{Cite web|title=【ﾑｰﾀﾞﾝ】李氏朝鮮VS琉球王国7【ﾕﾀ】|url=https://webcache.googleusercontent.com/search?q=cache:27J1abZ5nb0J:mimizun.com/log/2ch/korea/1347841276/|access-date=2024-01-06|website=webcache.googleusercontent.com}}</ref><ref>{{Cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=EL0nAAAAYAAJ&newbks=0&printsec=frontcover&dq=%22%E5%A4%A9%E6%9B%BD%E5%88%A9%E5%91%BD%22&q=%22%E5%A4%A9%E6%9B%BD%E5%88%A9%E5%91%BD%22&hl=en|title=古代氏族系譜集成|last=Hōga|first=Toshio|last2=宝賀寿男|date=1986|publisher=古代氏族研究会|language=zh-TW}}</ref><ref>[https://ndlsearch.ndl.go.jp/books/R100000002-I000002274014 古代豪族系図集覧]</ref>.
This means the {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Hayato people|en|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Hayato people}} used to believe they were descended from him.
== Explanation == He is said to be the older brother of {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Ahiratsu-hime|4=en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Ahiratsu-hime}} (吾平津媛), the first wife of [[Emperor Jimmu]]. <ref name="A109 天曽利命">{{Cite web|title=A109 天曽利命|url=https://www.his-trip.info/keizu/entry46.html|access-date=2023-12-14|website=www.his-trip.info|language=ja}}</ref>
Some people think he had an another name: Agatanushi Obashi (吾田君小橋) <ref name="sakura.ne.jp">{{Cite web|date=2016-03-19|title=�v�Ď��l|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160319134404/http://ek1010.sakura.ne.jp/1234-7-41.html|access-date=2024-01-06|website=web.archive.org}}</ref> .
It is possible that Tensori no Mikoto is {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Hosuseri|4=en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Hosuseri}}'s son <ref name="ﾑｰﾀﾞﾝ">{{Cite web|title=【ﾑｰﾀﾞﾝ】李氏朝鮮VS琉球王国7【ﾕﾀ】|url=https://webcache.googleusercontent.com/search?q=cache:27J1abZ5nb0J:mimizun.com/log/2ch/korea/1347841276/|access-date=2024-01-06|website=webcache.googleusercontent.com}}</ref> .

First edition published 1972. {{ISBN|978-0-8048-3674-6}}</ref><ref>"According to the 'Kojiki', the great 8th century A.D. compilation of Japanese mythology, Konohana Sakuya-hime married a god who grew suspicious of her when she became pregnant shortly after their wedding. The fire ceremony at Fuji-Yyoshida recalls this story as a means of protecting the town from fire and promoting easy childbirth among women."</ref> }}

{{Infobox person | name = Tensori no Mikoto | native_name = 天曽利命 | children = {{ill|Hayato people|en}} | father = {{ill|Hoderi|en}}<ref>''Nihongi: Chronicles of Japan from the Earliest Times to A.D. 697'', translated from the original Chinese and Japanese by [[William George Aston]]. Book II, page 73. Tuttle Publishing. Tra edition (July 2005). First edition published 1972. {{ISBN|978-0-8048-3674-6}}</ref><ref>"According to the 'Kojiki', the great 8th century A.D. compilation of Japanese mythology, Konohana Sakuya-hime married a god who grew suspicious of her when she became pregnant shortly after their wedding. To prove her fidelity to her husband, she entered a benign [[Bedroom|bower]] and miraculously gave birth to a son, unscathed by the surrounding flames. The fire ceremony at Fuji-Yyoshida recalls this story as a means of protecting the town from fire and promoting easy childbirth among women."</ref> }}
'''Tensori no Mikoto''' (天曽利命) a Shinto god. He is considered the {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Oyagami}} or ancestral god of the {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Hayato people|en|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Hayato people}}<ref name="A109 天曽利命">{{Cite web|title=A109 天曽利命|url=https://www.his-trip.info/keizu/entry46.html|access-date=2023-12-14|website=www.his-trip.info|language=ja}}</ref><ref name="sakura.ne.jp">{{Cite web|date=2016-03-19|title=�v�Ď��l|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160319134404/http://ek1010.sakura.ne.jp/1234-7-41.html|access-date=2024-01-06|website=web.archive.org}}</ref><ref name="ﾑｰﾀﾞﾝ">{{Cite web|title=【ﾑｰﾀﾞﾝ】李氏朝鮮VS琉球王国7【ﾕﾀ】|url=https://webcache.googleusercontent.com/search?q=cache:27J1abZ5nb0J:mimizun.com/log/2ch/korea/1347841276/|access-date=2024-01-06|website=webcache.googleusercontent.com}}</ref><ref>{{Cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=EL0nAAAAYAAJ&newbks=0&printsec=frontcover&dq=%22%E5%A4%A9%E6%9B%BD%E5%88%A9%E5%91%BD%22&q=%22%E5%A4%A9%E6%9B%BD%E5%88%A9%E5%91%BD%22&hl=en|title=古代氏族系譜集成|last=Hōga|first=Toshio|last2=宝賀寿男|date=1986|publisher=古代氏族研究会|language=zh-TW}}</ref><ref>[https://ndlsearch.ndl.go.jp/books/R100000002-I000002274014 古代豪族系図集覧]</ref>.
This means the {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Hayato people|en|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Hayato people}} used to believe they were descended from him.
== Explanation == He is said to be the older brother of {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Ahiratsu-hime|4=en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Ahiratsu-hime}} (吾平津媛), the first wife of [[Emperor Jimmu]]. <ref name="A109 天曽利命">{{Cite web|title=A109 天曽利命|url=https://www.his-trip.info/keizu/entry46.html|access-date=2023-12-14|website=www.his-trip.info|language=ja}}</ref>
Some people think he had an another name: Agatanushi Obashi (吾田君小橋) <ref name="sakura.ne.jp">{{Cite web|date=2016-03-19|title=�v�Ď��l|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160319134404/http://ek1010.sakura.ne.jp/1234-7-41.html|access-date=2024-01-06|website=web.archive.org}}</ref> .
It is possible that Tensori no Mikoto is {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Hosuseri|4=en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Hosuseri}}'s son <ref name="ﾑｰﾀﾞﾝ">{{Cite web|title=【ﾑｰﾀﾞﾝ】李氏朝鮮VS琉球王国7【ﾕﾀ】|url=https://webcache.googleusercontent.com/search?q=cache:27J1abZ5nb0J:mimizun.com/log/2ch/korea/1347841276/|access-date=2024-01-06|website=webcache.googleusercontent.com}}</ref> .
== Family tree == {{Three generations of Hyuga}}

First edition published 1972. {{ISBN|978-0-8048-3674-6}}</ref><ref>"According to the 'Kojiki', the great 8th century A.D. compilation of Japanese mythology, Konohana Sakuya-hime married a god who grew suspicious of her when she became pregnant shortly after their wedding. The fire ceremony at Fuji-Yyoshida recalls this story as a means of protecting the town from fire and promoting easy childbirth among women."</ref> }}

He is considered the {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Oyagami}} or ancestral god of the {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Hayato people|en|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Hayato people}}<ref name="A109 天曽利命">{{Cite web|title=A109 天曽利命|url=https://www.his-trip.info/keizu/entry46.html|access-date=2023-12-14|website=www.his-trip.info|language=ja}}</ref><ref name="sakura.ne.jp">{{Cite web|date=2016-03-19|title=�v�Ď��l|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160319134404/http://ek1010.sakura.ne.jp/1234-7-41.html|access-date=2024-01-06|website=web.archive.org}}</ref><ref name="ﾑｰﾀﾞﾝ">{{Cite web|title=【ﾑｰﾀﾞﾝ】李氏朝鮮VS琉球王国7【ﾕﾀ】|url=https://webcache.googleusercontent.com/search?q=cache:27J1abZ5nb0J:mimizun.com/log/2ch/korea/1347841276/|access-date=2024-01-06|website=webcache.googleusercontent.com}}</ref><ref>{{Cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=EL0nAAAAYAAJ&newbks=0&printsec=frontcover&dq=%22%E5%A4%A9%E6%9B%BD%E5%88%A9%E5%91%BD%22&q=%22%E5%A4%A9%E6%9B%BD%E5%88%A9%E5%91%BD%22&hl=en|title=古代氏族系譜集成|last=Hōga|first=Toshio|last2=宝賀寿男|date=1986|publisher=古代氏族研究会|language=zh-TW}}</ref><ref>[https://ndlsearch.ndl.go.jp/books/R100000002-I000002274014 古代豪族系図集覧]</ref>.
This means the {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Hayato people|en|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Hayato people}} used to believe they were descended from him.
== Explanation == He is said to be the older brother of {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Ahiratsu-hime|4=en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Ahiratsu-hime}} (吾平津媛), the first wife of [[Emperor Jimmu]]. <ref name="A109 天曽利命">{{Cite web|title=A109 天曽利命|url=https://www.his-trip.info/keizu/entry46.html|access-date=2023-12-14|website=www.his-trip.info|language=ja}}</ref>
Some people think he had an another name: Agatanushi Obashi (吾田君小橋) <ref name="sakura.ne.jp">{{Cite web|date=2016-03-19|title=�v�Ď��l|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160319134404/http://ek1010.sakura.ne.jp/1234-7-41.html|access-date=2024-01-06|website=web.archive.org}}</ref> .
It is possible that Tensori no Mikoto is {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Hosuseri|4=en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Hosuseri}}'s son <ref name="ﾑｰﾀﾞﾝ">{{Cite web|title=【ﾑｰﾀﾞﾝ】李氏朝鮮VS琉球王国7【ﾕﾀ】|url=https://webcache.googleusercontent.com/search?q=cache:27J1abZ5nb0J:mimizun.com/log/2ch/korea/1347841276/|access-date=2024-01-06|website=webcache.googleusercontent.com}}</ref> .

First edition published 1972. {{ISBN|978-0-8048-3674-6}}</ref><ref>"According to the 'Kojiki', the great 8th century A.D. compilation of Japanese mythology, Konohana Sakuya-hime married a god who grew suspicious of her when she became pregnant shortly after their wedding. The fire ceremony at Fuji-Yyoshida recalls this story as a means of protecting the town from fire and promoting easy childbirth among women."</ref> }}

He is considered the {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Oyagami}} or ancestral god of the {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Hayato people|en|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Hayato people}}<ref name="A109 天曽利命">{{Cite web|title=A109 天曽利命|url=https://www.his-trip.info/keizu/entry46.html|access-date=2023-12-14|website=www.his-trip.info|language=ja}}</ref><ref name="sakura.ne.jp">{{Cite web|date=2016-03-19|title=�v�Ď��l|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160319134404/http://ek1010.sakura.ne.jp/1234-7-41.html|access-date=2024-01-06|website=web.archive.org}}</ref><ref name="ﾑｰﾀﾞﾝ">{{Cite web|title=【ﾑｰﾀﾞﾝ】李氏朝鮮VS琉球王国7【ﾕﾀ】|url=https://webcache.googleusercontent.com/search?q=cache:27J1abZ5nb0J:mimizun.com/log/2ch/korea/1347841276/|access-date=2024-01-06|website=webcache.googleusercontent.com}}</ref><ref>{{Cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=EL0nAAAAYAAJ&newbks=0&printsec=frontcover&dq=%22%E5%A4%A9%E6%9B%BD%E5%88%A9%E5%91%BD%22&q=%22%E5%A4%A9%E6%9B%BD%E5%88%A9%E5%91%BD%22&hl=en|title=古代氏族系譜集成|last=Hōga|first=Toshio|last2=宝賀寿男|date=1986|publisher=古代氏族研究会|language=zh-TW}}</ref><ref>[https://ndlsearch.ndl.go.jp/books/R100000002-I000002274014 古代豪族系図集覧]</ref>.
This means the {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Hayato people|en|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Hayato people}} used to believe they were descended from him.
== Explanation == He is said to be the older brother of {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Ahiratsu-hime|4=en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Ahiratsu-hime}} (吾平津媛), the first wife of [[Emperor Jimmu]]. <ref name="A109 天曽利命">{{Cite web|title=A109 天曽利命|url=https://www.his-trip.info/keizu/entry46.html|access-date=2023-12-14|website=www.his-trip.info|language=ja}}</ref>
Some people think he had an another name: Agatanushi Obashi (吾田君小橋) <ref name="sakura.ne.jp">{{Cite web|date=2016-03-19|title=�v�Ď��l|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160319134404/http://ek1010.sakura.ne.jp/1234-7-41.html|access-date=2024-01-06|website=web.archive.org}}</ref> .
It is possible that Tensori no Mikoto is {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Hosuseri|4=en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Hosuseri}}'s son <ref name="ﾑｰﾀﾞﾝ">{{Cite web|title=【ﾑｰﾀﾞﾝ】李氏朝鮮VS琉球王国7【ﾕﾀ】|url=https://webcache.googleusercontent.com/search?q=cache:27J1abZ5nb0J:mimizun.com/log/2ch/korea/1347841276/|access-date=2024-01-06|website=webcache.googleusercontent.com}}</ref> .
== Family tree == {{Three generations of Hyuga}}
== Related articles ==
* {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Ahiratsu-hime|4=en}} * {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Oyagami}} * {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Oyagami}} du [[Azumi people|clan Azumi]] ** {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Utsushihikanasaku|5=en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Utsushihikanasaku|8=ja|9=宇都志日金拆命}} ** {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Azumi-no-isora|4=en}} * ''{{ill|Tenson-zoku|fr||ja|天孫族|zh|天孫民族|en:draft||simple|User:Immanuelle/Tenson-zoku|ko|천손족|uk|Тенсон Зоку|12=draft}}''

revision.text:
<!-- 削除についての議論が終了するまで、下記のメッセージ部分は除去しないでください。もしあなたがこのテンプレートを除去した場合、差し戻されます。またページが保護されることもあります。 --> {{Sakujo/本体|2025年4月28日|Immanuelleによる機械翻訳で作成された記事}} <!-- 削除についての議論が終了するまで、上記部分は削除しないでください。 --> {{複数の問題 | 精度 = 2024年2月2日 (金) 10:56 (UTC) | 正確性 = 2024年2月2日 (金) 10:56 (UTC) | Wikify = 2024年2月2日 (金) 10:56 (UTC) }} {{更新|date=2024年5月}} {{未検証|date=2024年2月}} {{Uncategorized|date=2024年2月}} '''天曽利命'''（てんそりのみこと）は[[隼人]]族の[[祖神]]であったとされる人物<ref name="A109 天曽利命">{{Cite web |title=A109 天曽利命 |url=https://www.his-trip.info/keizu/entry46.html |website=www.his-trip.info |access-date=2023-12-14 |language=ja}}</ref><ref name="sakura.ne.jp">{{Cite web |title=�v�Ď��l |url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160319134404/http://ek1010.sakura.ne.jp/1234-7-41.html |website=web.archive.org |date=2016-03-19 |access-date=2024-01-06}}</ref><ref>{{Cite book |title=古代氏族系譜集成 |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=EL0nAAAAYAAJ&newbks=0&printsec=frontcover&dq=%22%E5%A4%A9%E6%9B%BD%E5%88%A9%E5%91%BD%22&q=%22%E5%A4%A9%E6%9B%BD%E5%88%A9%E5%91%BD%22&hl=en |publisher=古代氏族研究会 |date=1986 |language=zh-TW |first=Toshio |last=Hōga |last2=宝賀寿男}}</ref><ref>[https://ndlsearch.ndl.go.jp/books/R100000002-I000002274014 古代豪族系図集覧]</ref>。
== 解説 == 彼は[[神武天皇]]の最初の妻である[[吾平津媛]]の兄だと言われています<ref name="A109 天曽利命" />。
天曽利命は時々、吾田君小橋と同一視されることがあります<ref name="sakura.ne.jp" />。
== 系譜 == {{日向三代}}
== 関連項目 == * [[吾平津媛]] * [[阿曇氏]]の[[祖神]] ** [[宇都志日金拆命]] ** [[阿曇磯良]]
== 脚注 == <references />
{{DEFAULTSORT:てんそりのみこと}} [[Category:日本の神 (人物神 記紀)]] [[Category:隼人]]

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:55, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}}
{{Infobox person
| name               = Tensori no Mikoto
| native_name        = 天曽利命
| children           = {{ill|Hayato people|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayato people|qq=}}|father             = {{ill|Hoderi|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hoderi|qq=}}<ref>''Nihongi: Chronicles of Japan from the Earliest Times to A.D. 697'', translated from the original Chinese and Japanese by [[William George Aston]]. Book II, page 73. Tuttle Publishing. Tra edition (July 2005). First edition published 1972. {{ISBN|978-0-8048-3674-6}}</ref><ref>"According to the 'Kojiki', the great 8th century A.D. compilation of Japanese mythology, Konohana Sakuya-hime married a god who grew suspicious of her when she became pregnant shortly after their wedding. To prove her fidelity to her husband, she entered a benign [[:en:Bedroom|bower]] and miraculously gave birth to a son, unscathed by the surrounding flames. The fire ceremony at Fuji-Yyoshida recalls this story as a means of protecting the town from fire and promoting easy childbirth among women."</ref>
}}

'''Tensori no Mikoto''' (天曽利命) a Shinto god. He is considered the {{ill|Oyagami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oyagami|qq=}} or ancestral god of the {{ill|Hayato people|en|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Hayato people|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayato people|qq=}}<ref name="A109 天曽利命">{{Cite web|title=A109 天曽利命|url=https://www.his-trip.info/keizu/entry46.html|access-date=2023-12-14|website=www.his-trip.info|language=ja}}</ref><ref name="sakura.ne.jp">{{Cite web|date=2016-03-19|title=�v�Ď��l|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160319134404/http://ek1010.sakura.ne.jp/1234-7-41.html|access-date=2024-01-06|website=web.archive.org}}</ref><ref name="ﾑｰﾀﾞﾝ">{{Cite web|title=【ﾑｰﾀﾞﾝ】李氏朝鮮VS琉球王国7【ﾕﾀ】|url=https://webcache.googleusercontent.com/search?q=cache:27J1abZ5nb0J:mimizun.com/log/2ch/korea/1347841276/|access-date=2024-01-06|website=webcache.googleusercontent.com}}</ref><ref>{{Cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=EL0nAAAAYAAJ&newbks=0&printsec=frontcover&dq=%22%E5%A4%A9%E6%9B%BD%E5%88%A9%E5%91%BD%22&q=%22%E5%A4%A9%E6%9B%BD%E5%88%A9%E5%91%BD%22&hl=en|title=古代氏族系譜集成|last=Hōga|first=Toshio|last2=宝賀寿男|date=1986|publisher=古代氏族研究会|language=zh-TW}}</ref><ref>[https://ndlsearch.ndl.go.jp/books/R100000002-I000002274014 古代豪族系図集覧]</ref>.

This means the {{ill|Hayato people|en|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Hayato people|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayato people|qq=}} used to believe they were descended from him.

== Explanation ==
He is said to be the older brother of {{ill|Ahiratsu-hime|4=en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Ahiratsu-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ahiratsu-hime|qq=}} (吾平津媛), the first wife of [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]]. <ref name="A109 天曽利命">{{Cite web|title=A109 天曽利命|url=https://www.his-trip.info/keizu/entry46.html|access-date=2023-12-14|website=www.his-trip.info|language=ja}}</ref>

Some people think he had an another name: Agatanushi Obashi (吾田君小橋) <ref name="sakura.ne.jp">{{Cite web|date=2016-03-19|title=�v�Ď��l|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160319134404/http://ek1010.sakura.ne.jp/1234-7-41.html|access-date=2024-01-06|website=web.archive.org}}</ref> .

It is possible that Tensori no Mikoto is {{ill|Hosuseri|4=en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Hosuseri|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hosuseri|qq=}}'s son <ref name="ﾑｰﾀﾞﾝ">{{Cite web|title=【ﾑｰﾀﾞﾝ】李氏朝鮮VS琉球王国7【ﾕﾀ】|url=https://webcache.googleusercontent.com/search?q=cache:27J1abZ5nb0J:mimizun.com/log/2ch/korea/1347841276/|access-date=2024-01-06|website=webcache.googleusercontent.com}}</ref> .

== Family tree ==
{{Three generations of Hyuga}}

'''Tensori no Mikoto''' (天曽利命) a Shinto god. He is considered the {{ill|Oyagami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oyagami|qq=}} or ancestral god of the {{ill|Hayato people|en|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Hayato people|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayato people|qq=}}<ref name="A109 天曽利命">{{Cite web|title=A109 天曽利命|url=https://www.his-trip.info/keizu/entry46.html|access-date=2023-12-14|website=www.his-trip.info|language=ja}}</ref><ref name="sakura.ne.jp">{{Cite web|date=2016-03-19|title=�v�Ď��l|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160319134404/http://ek1010.sakura.ne.jp/1234-7-41.html|access-date=2024-01-06|website=web.archive.org}}</ref><ref name="ﾑｰﾀﾞﾝ">{{Cite web|title=【ﾑｰﾀﾞﾝ】李氏朝鮮VS琉球王国7【ﾕﾀ】|url=https://webcache.googleusercontent.com/search?q=cache:27J1abZ5nb0J:mimizun.com/log/2ch/korea/1347841276/|access-date=2024-01-06|website=webcache.googleusercontent.com}}</ref><ref>{{Cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=EL0nAAAAYAAJ&newbks=0&printsec=frontcover&dq=%22%E5%A4%A9%E6%9B%BD%E5%88%A9%E5%91%BD%22&q=%22%E5%A4%A9%E6%9B%BD%E5%88%A9%E5%91%BD%22&hl=en|title=古代氏族系譜集成|last=Hōga|first=Toshio|last2=宝賀寿男|date=1986|publisher=古代氏族研究会|language=zh-TW}}</ref><ref>[https://ndlsearch.ndl.go.jp/books/R100000002-I000002274014 古代豪族系図集覧]</ref>.

This means the {{ill|Hayato people|en|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Hayato people|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayato people|qq=}} used to believe they were descended from him.

== Explanation ==
He is said to be the older brother of {{ill|Ahiratsu-hime|4=en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Ahiratsu-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ahiratsu-hime|qq=}} (吾平津媛), the first wife of [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]]. <ref name="A109 天曽利命">{{Cite web|title=A109 天曽利命|url=https://www.his-trip.info/keizu/entry46.html|access-date=2023-12-14|website=www.his-trip.info|language=ja}}</ref>

Some people think he had an another name: Agatanushi Obashi (吾田君小橋) <ref name="sakura.ne.jp">{{Cite web|date=2016-03-19|title=�v�Ď��l|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160319134404/http://ek1010.sakura.ne.jp/1234-7-41.html|access-date=2024-01-06|website=web.archive.org}}</ref> .

It is possible that Tensori no Mikoto is {{ill|Hosuseri|4=en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Hosuseri|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hosuseri|qq=}}'s son <ref name="ﾑｰﾀﾞﾝ">{{Cite web|title=【ﾑｰﾀﾞﾝ】李氏朝鮮VS琉球王国7【ﾕﾀ】|url=https://webcache.googleusercontent.com/search?q=cache:27J1abZ5nb0J:mimizun.com/log/2ch/korea/1347841276/|access-date=2024-01-06|website=webcache.googleusercontent.com}}</ref> .

{{Infobox person
| name               = Tensori no Mikoto
| native_name        = 天曽利命
| children           = {{ill|Hayato people|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayato people|qq=}}|father             = {{ill|Hoderi|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hoderi|qq=}}<ref>''Nihongi: Chronicles of Japan from the Earliest Times to A.D. 697'', translated from the original Chinese and Japanese by [[William George Aston]]. Book II, page 73. Tuttle Publishing. Tra edition (July 2005). First edition published 1972. {{ISBN|978-0-8048-3674-6}}</ref><ref>"According to the 'Kojiki', the great 8th century A.D. compilation of Japanese mythology, Konohana Sakuya-hime married a god who grew suspicious of her when she became pregnant shortly after their wedding. To prove her fidelity to her husband, she entered a benign [[:en:Bedroom|bower]] and miraculously gave birth to a son, unscathed by the surrounding flames. The fire ceremony at Fuji-Yyoshida recalls this story as a means of protecting the town from fire and promoting easy childbirth among women."</ref>
}}

'''Tensori no Mikoto''' (天曽利命) a Shinto god. He is considered the {{ill|Oyagami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oyagami|qq=}} or ancestral god of the {{ill|Hayato people|en|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Hayato people|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayato people|qq=}}<ref name="A109 天曽利命">{{Cite web|title=A109 天曽利命|url=https://www.his-trip.info/keizu/entry46.html|access-date=2023-12-14|website=www.his-trip.info|language=ja}}</ref><ref name="sakura.ne.jp">{{Cite web|date=2016-03-19|title=�v�Ď��l|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160319134404/http://ek1010.sakura.ne.jp/1234-7-41.html|access-date=2024-01-06|website=web.archive.org}}</ref><ref name="ﾑｰﾀﾞﾝ">{{Cite web|title=【ﾑｰﾀﾞﾝ】李氏朝鮮VS琉球王国7【ﾕﾀ】|url=https://webcache.googleusercontent.com/search?q=cache:27J1abZ5nb0J:mimizun.com/log/2ch/korea/1347841276/|access-date=2024-01-06|website=webcache.googleusercontent.com}}</ref><ref>{{Cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=EL0nAAAAYAAJ&newbks=0&printsec=frontcover&dq=%22%E5%A4%A9%E6%9B%BD%E5%88%A9%E5%91%BD%22&q=%22%E5%A4%A9%E6%9B%BD%E5%88%A9%E5%91%BD%22&hl=en|title=古代氏族系譜集成|last=Hōga|first=Toshio|last2=宝賀寿男|date=1986|publisher=古代氏族研究会|language=zh-TW}}</ref><ref>[https://ndlsearch.ndl.go.jp/books/R100000002-I000002274014 古代豪族系図集覧]</ref>.

This means the {{ill|Hayato people|en|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Hayato people|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayato people|qq=}} used to believe they were descended from him.

== Explanation ==
He is said to be the older brother of {{ill|Ahiratsu-hime|4=en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Ahiratsu-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ahiratsu-hime|qq=}} (吾平津媛), the first wife of [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]]. <ref name="A109 天曽利命">{{Cite web|title=A109 天曽利命|url=https://www.his-trip.info/keizu/entry46.html|access-date=2023-12-14|website=www.his-trip.info|language=ja}}</ref>

Some people think he had an another name: Agatanushi Obashi (吾田君小橋) <ref name="sakura.ne.jp">{{Cite web|date=2016-03-19|title=�v�Ď��l|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160319134404/http://ek1010.sakura.ne.jp/1234-7-41.html|access-date=2024-01-06|website=web.archive.org}}</ref> .

It is possible that Tensori no Mikoto is {{ill|Hosuseri|4=en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Hosuseri|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hosuseri|qq=}}'s son <ref name="ﾑｰﾀﾞﾝ">{{Cite web|title=【ﾑｰﾀﾞﾝ】李氏朝鮮VS琉球王国7【ﾕﾀ】|url=https://webcache.googleusercontent.com/search?q=cache:27J1abZ5nb0J:mimizun.com/log/2ch/korea/1347841276/|access-date=2024-01-06|website=webcache.googleusercontent.com}}</ref> .

It is possible that Tensori no Mikoto is {{ill|Hosuseri|4=en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Hosuseri|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hosuseri|qq=}}'s son <ref name="ﾑｰﾀﾞﾝ">{{Cite web|title=【ﾑｰﾀﾞﾝ】李氏朝鮮VS琉球王国7【ﾕﾀ】|url=https://webcache.googleusercontent.com/search?q=cache:27J1abZ5nb0J:mimizun.com/log/2ch/korea/1347841276/|access-date=2024-01-06|website=webcache.googleusercontent.com}}</ref> .

{{Infobox person
| name               = Tensori no Mikoto

| children           = {{ill|Hayato people|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayato people|qq=}}|father             = {{ill|Hoderi|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hoderi|qq=}}<ref>''Nihongi: Chronicles of Japan from the Earliest Times to A.D. 697'', translated from the original Chinese and Japanese by [[William George Aston]]. Book II, page 73. Tuttle Publishing. Tra edition (July 2005). First edition published 1972. {{ISBN|978-0-8048-3674-6}}</ref><ref>"According to the 'Kojiki', the great 8th century A.D. compilation of Japanese mythology, Konohana Sakuya-hime married a god who grew suspicious of her when she became pregnant shortly after their wedding. To prove her fidelity to her husband, she entered a benign [[:en:Bedroom|bower]] and miraculously gave birth to a son, unscathed by the surrounding flames. The fire ceremony at Fuji-Yyoshida recalls this story as a means of protecting the town from fire and promoting easy childbirth among women."</ref>
}}

'''Tensori no Mikoto''' (天曽利命) a Shinto god. He is considered the {{ill|Oyagami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oyagami|qq=}} or ancestral god of the {{ill|Hayato people|en|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Hayato people|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayato people|qq=}}<ref name="A109 天曽利命">{{Cite web|title=A109 天曽利命|url=https://www.his-trip.info/keizu/entry46.html|access-date=2023-12-14|website=www.his-trip.info|language=ja}}</ref><ref name="sakura.ne.jp">{{Cite web|date=2016-03-19|title=�v�Ď��l|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160319134404/http://ek1010.sakura.ne.jp/1234-7-41.html|access-date=2024-01-06|website=web.archive.org}}</ref><ref name="ﾑｰﾀﾞﾝ">{{Cite web|title=【ﾑｰﾀﾞﾝ】李氏朝鮮VS琉球王国7【ﾕﾀ】|url=https://webcache.googleusercontent.com/search?q=cache:27J1abZ5nb0J:mimizun.com/log/2ch/korea/1347841276/|access-date=2024-01-06|website=webcache.googleusercontent.com}}</ref><ref>{{Cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=EL0nAAAAYAAJ&newbks=0&printsec=frontcover&dq=%22%E5%A4%A9%E6%9B%BD%E5%88%A9%E5%91%BD%22&q=%22%E5%A4%A9%E6%9B%BD%E5%88%A9%E5%91%BD%22&hl=en|title=古代氏族系譜集成|last=Hōga|first=Toshio|last2=宝賀寿男|date=1986|publisher=古代氏族研究会|language=zh-TW}}</ref><ref>[https://ndlsearch.ndl.go.jp/books/R100000002-I000002274014 古代豪族系図集覧]</ref>.

This means the {{ill|Hayato people|en|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Hayato people|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayato people|qq=}} used to believe they were descended from him.

== Explanation ==
He is said to be the older brother of {{ill|Ahiratsu-hime|4=en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Ahiratsu-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ahiratsu-hime|qq=}} (吾平津媛), the first wife of [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]]. <ref name="A109 天曽利命">{{Cite web|title=A109 天曽利命|url=https://www.his-trip.info/keizu/entry46.html|access-date=2023-12-14|website=www.his-trip.info|language=ja}}</ref>

Some people think he had an another name: Agatanushi Obashi (吾田君小橋) <ref name="sakura.ne.jp">{{Cite web|date=2016-03-19|title=�v�Ď��l|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160319134404/http://ek1010.sakura.ne.jp/1234-7-41.html|access-date=2024-01-06|website=web.archive.org}}</ref> .

It is possible that Tensori no Mikoto is {{ill|Hosuseri|4=en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Hosuseri|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hosuseri|qq=}}'s son <ref name="ﾑｰﾀﾞﾝ">{{Cite web|title=【ﾑｰﾀﾞﾝ】李氏朝鮮VS琉球王国7【ﾕﾀ】|url=https://webcache.googleusercontent.com/search?q=cache:27J1abZ5nb0J:mimizun.com/log/2ch/korea/1347841276/|access-date=2024-01-06|website=webcache.googleusercontent.com}}</ref> .

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:31, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}}
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:55, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}}
{{Infobox person
| name               = Tensori no Mikoto
| native_name        = 天曽利命
| children           = {{ill|Hayato people|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayato people|qq=}}|father             = {{ill|Hoderi|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hoderi|qq=}}<ref>''Nihongi: Chronicles of Japan from the Earliest Times to A.D. 697'', translated from the original Chinese and Japanese by [[William George Aston]]. Book II, page 73. Tuttle Publishing. Tra edition (July 2005). First edition published 1972. {{ISBN|978-0-8048-3674-6}}</ref><ref>"According to the 'Kojiki', the great 8th century A.D. compilation of Japanese mythology, Konohana Sakuya-hime married a god who grew suspicious of her when she became pregnant shortly after their wedding. To prove her fidelity to her husband, she entered a benign [[:en:Bedroom|bower]] and miraculously gave birth to a son, unscathed by the surrounding flames. The fire ceremony at Fuji-Yyoshida recalls this story as a means of protecting the town from fire and promoting easy childbirth among women."</ref>
}}

'''Tensori no Mikoto''' (天曽利命) a Shinto god. He is considered the {{ill|Oyagami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oyagami|qq=}} or ancestral god of the {{ill|Hayato people|en|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Hayato people|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayato people|qq=}}<ref name="A109 天曽利命">{{Cite web|title=A109 天曽利命|url=https://www.his-trip.info/keizu/entry46.html|access-date=2023-12-14|website=www.his-trip.info|language=ja}}</ref><ref name="sakura.ne.jp">{{Cite web|date=2016-03-19|title=�v�Ď��l|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160319134404/http://ek1010.sakura.ne.jp/1234-7-41.html|access-date=2024-01-06|website=web.archive.org}}</ref><ref name="ﾑｰﾀﾞﾝ">{{Cite web|title=【ﾑｰﾀﾞﾝ】李氏朝鮮VS琉球王国7【ﾕﾀ】|url=https://webcache.googleusercontent.com/search?q=cache:27J1abZ5nb0J:mimizun.com/log/2ch/korea/1347841276/|access-date=2024-01-06|website=webcache.googleusercontent.com}}</ref><ref>{{Cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=EL0nAAAAYAAJ&newbks=0&printsec=frontcover&dq=%22%E5%A4%A9%E6%9B%BD%E5%88%A9%E5%91%BD%22&q=%22%E5%A4%A9%E6%9B%BD%E5%88%A9%E5%91%BD%22&hl=en|title=古代氏族系譜集成|last=Hōga|first=Toshio|last2=宝賀寿男|date=1986|publisher=古代氏族研究会|language=zh-TW}}</ref><ref>[https://ndlsearch.ndl.go.jp/books/R100000002-I000002274014 古代豪族系図集覧]</ref>.

This means the {{ill|Hayato people|en|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Hayato people|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayato people|qq=}} used to believe they were descended from him.

== Explanation ==
He is said to be the older brother of {{ill|Ahiratsu-hime|4=en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Ahiratsu-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ahiratsu-hime|qq=}} (吾平津媛), the first wife of [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]]. <ref name="A109 天曽利命">{{Cite web|title=A109 天曽利命|url=https://www.his-trip.info/keizu/entry46.html|access-date=2023-12-14|website=www.his-trip.info|language=ja}}</ref>

Some people think he had an another name: Agatanushi Obashi (吾田君小橋) <ref name="sakura.ne.jp">{{Cite web|date=2016-03-19|title=�v�Ď��l|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160319134404/http://ek1010.sakura.ne.jp/1234-7-41.html|access-date=2024-01-06|website=web.archive.org}}</ref> .

It is possible that Tensori no Mikoto is {{ill|Hosuseri|4=en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Hosuseri|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hosuseri|qq=}}'s son <ref name="ﾑｰﾀﾞﾝ">{{Cite web|title=【ﾑｰﾀﾞﾝ】李氏朝鮮VS琉球王国7【ﾕﾀ】|url=https://webcache.googleusercontent.com/search?q=cache:27J1abZ5nb0J:mimizun.com/log/2ch/korea/1347841276/|access-date=2024-01-06|website=webcache.googleusercontent.com}}</ref> .

== Family tree ==
{{Three generations of Hyuga}}

== Related articles ==

* {{ill|Ahiratsu-hime|4=en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ahiratsu-hime|qq=}}
* {{ill|Oyagami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oyagami|qq=}}
* {{ill|Oyagami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oyagami|qq=}} du [[Azumi people|clan Azumi]]
** {{ill|Utsushihikanasaku|5=en:draft|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Utsushihikanasaku|8=ja|9=宇都志日金拆命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsushihikanasaku|qq=}}
** {{ill|Azumi-no-isora|4=en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Azumi-no-isora|qq=}}
* ''{{ill|Tenson-zoku|fr|ja|天孫族|zh|天孫民族|en:draft|simple|User:Immanuelle/Tenson-zoku|ko|천손족|uk|Тенсон Зоку|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenson-zoku|qq=}}''

This means the {{ill|Hayato people|en|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Hayato people|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayato people|qq=}} used to believe they were descended from him.

Book II, page 73. Tuttle Publishing. Tra edition (July 2005). First edition published 1972. {{ISBN|978-0-8048-3674-6}}</ref><ref>"According to the 'Kojiki', the great 8th century A.D. compilation of Japanese mythology, Konohana Sakuya-hime married a god who grew suspicious of her when she became pregnant shortly after their wedding. To prove her fidelity to her husband, she entered a benign [[:en:Bedroom|bower]] and miraculously gave birth to a son, unscathed by the surrounding flames. The fire ceremony at Fuji-Yyoshida recalls this story as a means of protecting the town from fire and promoting easy childbirth among women."</ref>
}}

'''Tensori no Mikoto''' (天曽利命) a Shinto god. He is considered the {{ill|Oyagami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oyagami|qq=}} or ancestral god of the {{ill|Hayato people|en|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Hayato people|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayato people|qq=}}<ref name="A109 天曽利命">{{Cite web|title=A109 天曽利命|url=https://www.his-trip.info/keizu/entry46.html|access-date=2023-12-14|website=www.his-trip.info|language=ja}}</ref><ref name="sakura.ne.jp">{{Cite web|date=2016-03-19|title=�v�Ď��l|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160319134404/http://ek1010.sakura.ne.jp/1234-7-41.html|access-date=2024-01-06|website=web.archive.org}}</ref><ref name="ﾑｰﾀﾞﾝ">{{Cite web|title=【ﾑｰﾀﾞﾝ】李氏朝鮮VS琉球王国7【ﾕﾀ】|url=https://webcache.googleusercontent.com/search?q=cache:27J1abZ5nb0J:mimizun.com/log/2ch/korea/1347841276/|access-date=2024-01-06|website=webcache.googleusercontent.com}}</ref><ref>{{Cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=EL0nAAAAYAAJ&newbks=0&printsec=frontcover&dq=%22%E5%A4%A9%E6%9B%BD%E5%88%A9%E5%91%BD%22&q=%22%E5%A4%A9%E6%9B%BD%E5%88%A9%E5%91%BD%22&hl=en|title=古代氏族系譜集成|last=Hōga|first=Toshio|last2=宝賀寿男|date=1986|publisher=古代氏族研究会|language=zh-TW}}</ref><ref>[https://ndlsearch.ndl.go.jp/books/R100000002-I000002274014 古代豪族系図集覧]</ref>.

This means the {{ill|Hayato people|en|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Hayato people|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayato people|qq=}} used to believe they were descended from him.

== Explanation ==
He is said to be the older brother of {{ill|Ahiratsu-hime|4=en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Ahiratsu-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ahiratsu-hime|qq=}} (吾平津媛), the first wife of [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]]. <ref name="A109 天曽利命">{{Cite web|title=A109 天曽利命|url=https://www.his-trip.info/keizu/entry46.html|access-date=2023-12-14|website=www.his-trip.info|language=ja}}</ref>

'''Tensori no Mikoto''' (天曽利命) a Shinto god. He is considered the {{ill|Oyagami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oyagami|qq=}} or ancestral god of the {{ill|Hayato people|en|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Hayato people|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayato people|qq=}}<ref name="A109 天曽利命">{{Cite web|title=A109 天曽利命|url=https://www.his-trip.info/keizu/entry46.html|access-date=2023-12-14|website=www.his-trip.info|language=ja}}</ref><ref name="sakura.ne.jp">{{Cite web|date=2016-03-19|title=�v�Ď��l|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160319134404/http://ek1010.sakura.ne.jp/1234-7-41.html|access-date=2024-01-06|website=web.archive.org}}</ref><ref name="ﾑｰﾀﾞﾝ">{{Cite web|title=【ﾑｰﾀﾞﾝ】李氏朝鮮VS琉球王国7【ﾕﾀ】|url=https://webcache.googleusercontent.com/search?q=cache:27J1abZ5nb0J:mimizun.com/log/2ch/korea/1347841276/|access-date=2024-01-06|website=webcache.googleusercontent.com}}</ref><ref>{{Cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=EL0nAAAAYAAJ&newbks=0&printsec=frontcover&dq=%22%E5%A4%A9%E6%9B%BD%E5%88%A9%E5%91%BD%22&q=%22%E5%A4%A9%E6%9B%BD%E5%88%A9%E5%91%BD%22&hl=en|title=古代氏族系譜集成|last=Hōga|first=Toshio|last2=宝賀寿男|date=1986|publisher=古代氏族研究会|language=zh-TW}}</ref><ref>[https://ndlsearch.ndl.go.jp/books/R100000002-I000002274014 古代豪族系図集覧]</ref>.

This means the {{ill|Hayato people|en|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Hayato people|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayato people|qq=}} used to believe they were descended from him.

== Explanation ==
He is said to be the older brother of {{ill|Ahiratsu-hime|4=en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Ahiratsu-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ahiratsu-hime|qq=}} (吾平津媛), the first wife of [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]]. <ref name="A109 天曽利命">{{Cite web|title=A109 天曽利命|url=https://www.his-trip.info/keizu/entry46.html|access-date=2023-12-14|website=www.his-trip.info|language=ja}}</ref>

Some people think he had an another name: Agatanushi Obashi (吾田君小橋) <ref name="sakura.ne.jp">{{Cite web|date=2016-03-19|title=�v�Ď��l|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160319134404/http://ek1010.sakura.ne.jp/1234-7-41.html|access-date=2024-01-06|website=web.archive.org}}</ref> .

It is possible that Tensori no Mikoto is {{ill|Hosuseri|4=en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Hosuseri|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hosuseri|qq=}}'s son <ref name="ﾑｰﾀﾞﾝ">{{Cite web|title=【ﾑｰﾀﾞﾝ】李氏朝鮮VS琉球王国7【ﾕﾀ】|url=https://webcache.googleusercontent.com/search?q=cache:27J1abZ5nb0J:mimizun.com/log/2ch/korea/1347841276/|access-date=2024-01-06|website=webcache.googleusercontent.com}}</ref> .

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:55, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}}
{{Infobox person
| name               = Tensori no Mikoto
| native_name        = 天曽利命
| children           = {{ill|Hayato people|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayato people|qq=}}|father             = {{ill|Hoderi|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hoderi|qq=}}<ref>''Nihongi: Chronicles of Japan from the Earliest Times to A.D. 697'', translated from the original Chinese and Japanese by [[William George Aston]]. Book II, page 73. Tuttle Publishing. Tra edition (July 2005). First edition published 1972. {{ISBN|978-0-8048-3674-6}}</ref><ref>"According to the 'Kojiki', the great 8th century A.D. compilation of Japanese mythology, Konohana Sakuya-hime married a god who grew suspicious of her when she became pregnant shortly after their wedding. To prove her fidelity to her husband, she entered a benign {{ill|Bedroom|en|Bedroom|WD=Q193837|lt=bower}} and miraculously gave birth to a son, unscathed by the surrounding flames. The fire ceremony at Fuji-Yyoshida recalls this story as a means of protecting the town from fire and promoting easy childbirth among women."</ref>
}}

'''Tensori no Mikoto''' (天曽利命) a Shinto god. He is considered the {{ill|Oyagami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oyagami|qq=}} or ancestral god of the {{ill|Hayato people|en|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Hayato people|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayato people|qq=}}<ref name="A109 天曽利命">{{Cite web|title=A109 天曽利命|url=https://www.his-trip.info/keizu/entry46.html|access-date=2023-12-14|website=www.his-trip.info|language=ja}}</ref><ref name="sakura.ne.jp">{{Cite web|date=2016-03-19|title=�v�Ď��l|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160319134404/http://ek1010.sakura.ne.jp/1234-7-41.html|access-date=2024-01-06|website=web.archive.org}}</ref><ref name="ﾑｰﾀﾞﾝ">{{Cite web|title=【ﾑｰﾀﾞﾝ】李氏朝鮮VS琉球王国7【ﾕﾀ】|url=https://webcache.googleusercontent.com/search?q=cache:27J1abZ5nb0J:mimizun.com/log/2ch/korea/1347841276/|access-date=2024-01-06|website=webcache.googleusercontent.com}}</ref><ref>{{Cite book|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=EL0nAAAAYAAJ&newbks=0&printsec=frontcover&dq=%22%E5%A4%A9%E6%9B%BD%E5%88%A9%E5%91%BD%22&q=%22%E5%A4%A9%E6%9B%BD%E5%88%A9%E5%91%BD%22&hl=en|title=古代氏族系譜集成|last=Hōga|first=Toshio|last2=宝賀寿男|date=1986|publisher=古代氏族研究会|language=zh-TW}}</ref><ref>[https://ndlsearch.ndl.go.jp/books/R100000002-I000002274014 古代豪族系図集覧]</ref>.

This means the {{ill|Hayato people|en|4=simple|5=User:Immanuelle/Hayato people|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayato people|qq=}} used to believe they were descended from him.

== Explanation ==
He is said to be the older brother of {{ill|Ahiratsu-hime|4=en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Ahiratsu-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ahiratsu-hime|qq=}} (吾平津媛), the first wife of {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|en|Emperor Jimmu|WD=Q200188|lt=Emperor Jimmu}}. <ref name="A109 天曽利命">{{Cite web|title=A109 天曽利命|url=https://www.his-trip.info/keizu/entry46.html|access-date=2023-12-14|website=www.his-trip.info|language=ja}}</ref>

Some people think he had an another name: Agatanushi Obashi (吾田君小橋) <ref name="sakura.ne.jp">{{Cite web|date=2016-03-19|title=�v�Ď��l|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160319134404/http://ek1010.sakura.ne.jp/1234-7-41.html|access-date=2024-01-06|website=web.archive.org}}</ref> .

It is possible that Tensori no Mikoto is {{ill|Hosuseri|4=en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Hosuseri|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hosuseri|qq=}}'s son <ref name="ﾑｰﾀﾞﾝ">{{Cite web|title=【ﾑｰﾀﾞﾝ】李氏朝鮮VS琉球王国7【ﾕﾀ】|url=https://webcache.googleusercontent.com/search?q=cache:27J1abZ5nb0J:mimizun.com/log/2ch/korea/1347841276/|access-date=2024-01-06|website=webcache.googleusercontent.com}}</ref> .

== Family tree ==
{{Three generations of Hyuga}}

He is said to be the older brother of {{ill|Ahiratsu-hime|4=en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Ahiratsu-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ahiratsu-hime|qq=}} (吾平津媛), the first wife of {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|en|Emperor Jimmu|WD=Q200188|lt=Emperor Jimmu}}. <ref name="A109 天曽利命">{{Cite web|title=A109 天曽利命|url=https://www.his-trip.info/keizu/entry46.html|access-date=2023-12-14|website=www.his-trip.info|language=ja}}</ref>

Some people think he had an another name: Agatanushi Obashi (吾田君小橋) <ref name="sakura.ne.jp">{{Cite web|date=2016-03-19|title=�v�Ď��l|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160319134404/http://ek1010.sakura.ne.jp/1234-7-41.html|access-date=2024-01-06|website=web.archive.org}}</ref> .

It is possible that Tensori no Mikoto is {{ill|Hosuseri|4=en|6=simple|7=User:Immanuelle/Hosuseri|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hosuseri|qq=}}'s son <ref name="ﾑｰﾀﾞﾝ">{{Cite web|title=【ﾑｰﾀﾞﾝ】李氏朝鮮VS琉球王国7【ﾕﾀ】|url=https://webcache.googleusercontent.com/search?q=cache:27J1abZ5nb0J:mimizun.com/log/2ch/korea/1347841276/|access-date=2024-01-06|website=webcache.googleusercontent.com}}</ref> .

First edition published 1972. {{ISBN|978-0-8048-3674-6}}</ref><ref>"According to the 'Kojiki', the great 8th century A.D. compilation of Japanese mythology, Konohana Sakuya-hime married a god who grew suspicious of her when she became pregnant shortly after their wedding. }}
